observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan and Local Development Council continue complying with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011 and (a) invest in high-impact capital expenditure projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund;  and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive and heads of concerned implementing offices (i) implement without further delay the remaining projects not completed/implemented in CY 2021; and (ii) continue to monitor and supervise the effective and efficient implementation of projects funded from various source agencies within the timelines of the MOA, as far as feasible; and (b) Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer continue to be prompt in the return of excess funds to the source agencies upon completion or termination of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require members of the Local School Board (LSB) to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 on SEF utilization, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, and copy furnish the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG to promote and enhance transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the SWM Focal Person, (a) establish MRF in the 11 barangays to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level; (b) coordinate and require all barangays located in the coastal areas to have proper solid waste management disposal and closely monitor their implementation; and (c) instruct the barangay officials to inform their barangay collectors/segregators/ sorters to properly sort the wastes they had collected before transferring them to the Central MRF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) MDRRMC to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP indicating the (i) detailed PPAs and the corresponding appropriation classified as either Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) or Capital Outlay (CO); (ii) under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years for ease of monitoring project implementation; (iii) ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation of projects and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i)  maintain corresponding Subsidiary Ledger for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment; and (ii) ensure that the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF is discussed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 particularly on the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the Municipality.

However, we also recommended that under the guidance of the LCE, the Treasury’s Officer and personnel continue to enforce collection remedies as allowed by the pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of Contracts, Purchase Orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.

We commended the Management and its personnel for the effort to trace the unreconciled prior years’ balance.  However, we recommended that the officers and personnel concerned:
sustain efforts to remit amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations; and
exert efforts to completely trace and reconcile the unremitted prior years’ balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
exert best efforts to reconcile employees’ records at HDMF to be able to trace the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to Pag-IBIG account;
check with the records whether all Pag-IBIG premiums, Multi-Purpose Loan repayments and Housing Loan repayments deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all employees were remitted; and
cause the immediate remittance to Pag-IBIG of the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees, as warranted by the result of inquiry and reconciliation with HDMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) continue to comply with the deduction of PhilHealth contributions of its employees; and (b) promptly remit all deductions made pursuant to Section 18(b) of RA No. 7875 and Its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

observations.reason: Management disclosed that at the time of the application for loan of a permanent employee from the Land Bank, HDMF, GSIS and other Government Financial Institutions (GFIs), the net take home pay of said employee meets the minimum amount of P5,000.00. However, as soon as the monthly loan amortization is withheld from the salary of concerned employee, the net take home pay may be below P5,000.00. In such case, the employee supposedly could no longer avail of loans. Neither the Accountant nor the Human Resource Management Officer approves the application of loan if the net take home pay is below P5,000.00. Besides, loans applied thru the GIFs system will not be approved by the GIFs since they are likewise covered by existing regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require members of the Local School Board (LSB) to (a) submit quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization, using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, and copy furnish the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG to promote and enhance transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF; (b) come up with a strategic prioritization plan in the allocation of the SEF to public schools taking into consideration the most important needs of the students and not just for repairs of school facilities for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund in accordance with DepEd-DDBM-DLG JC 2017-001; and (c) incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for early Childhood Care Development.

6.8.	We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive, Sangguniang Bayan and Local Development Council continue complying with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011 and (a) to invest in high-impact capital expenditure projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund  and refrain from proposing non-capital investment projects under the Fund;  and (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

7.12.	We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive and heads of concerned implementing offices (i) implement without further delay the remaining projects not completed/implemented in CY 2019; and (ii) continue to monitor and supervise the effective and efficient implementation of projects funded from various source agencies within the timelines of the MOA, as far as feasible; and (b) Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer continue to be prompt in the return of excess funds to the source agencies upon completion or termination of the project.

8.14.	We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, through the SWM Focal Person, specifically undertake the following (a) establish MRF in the 11 barangays to facilitate the segregation of solid waste at the barangay level; (b) coordinate and require all barangays located in the coastal areas to have a proper solid waste management disposal and closely monitor their implementation; and  (c) instruct the barangay officials to inform their barangay collectors/segregators/ sorters to properly sort the wastes they had collected before transferring them to the Central MRF.

11.10.	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 particularly on the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the Municipality.

12.7.	We recommended that the Management stop the payment of RATA to SB members who fail to attend the weekly sessions and require the SB to refund the excess RATA received in view of absences incurred.
12.8.	We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer secure a certification from the SB Secretary as to the attendance of the members of the Honorable Body as a basis for the payment of RATA.

13.14.	We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Accountant to require that all the purchases of drugs and medicines be supported with the required certification by the requisitioning officers in accordance with PNDF Volume I to ensure compliance with EO No. 49;  and (b) require the concerned personnel of the MHO and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to closely comply with the rules and regulations on the procurement of drugs and medicines to ensure availability of safe, efficacious, and quality drugs at affordable cost.
13.15.	We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the members of the Bids and Awards Committee, including the BAC Secretariat, to (a) render an explanation or justification on the resort to shopping instead of thru competitive bidding in the procurement of the subject contracts; (b) henceforth, adhere to Sections 48.2 and 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts; and (c) resort only to alternative modes of procurement if the conditions are met as provided under Section 48, 52, and 53.9 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.

14.6.	We recommended that the General Services Office procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed using the prescribed alternative modes of procurement.

15.11.	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in recording all procured goods under the appropriate inventory account; (b) designated supply officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively, for each item; and (c) requisitioning officer and the supply officer to prepare RIS upon release of the items and prepare the SSMI and forward the same  to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of JEV to record the expenditures.

16.6.	We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the audit team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

17.5.	We recommend that management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from the personnel’s payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors.

18.3.	We commend Management and we recommend that it:
sustain its efforts in  remitting amounts due to the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations; and
exert efforts to trace and reconcile unremitted prior years’ balance.

19.5.	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
exert best efforts to reconcile employees’ records at HDMF to be able to trace the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to Pag-IBIG account, if any;
check with the records whether all Pag-IBIG premiums, Multi-Purpose Loan repayments and Housing Loan repayments deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all employees were remitted; and
cause the immediate remittance to Pag-IBIG for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees, as warranted by the result of inquiry and reconciliation with HDMF.

20.3.	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (a) continue to comply with the deduction of PhilHealth contributions of its employees; and (b) promptly remit all deductions made pursuant to Section 18(b) of RA No. 7875 and Its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

21.4.	We recommend that the Management continue to withhold taxes and check that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to the BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert best efforts to reconcile employees’ records at HDMF to be able to trace the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to Pag-IBIG account, if any; (b) check with the records whether all Pag-IBIG premiums, Multi-Purpose Loan repayments and Housing Loan repayments deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all employees were remitted; and (c) cause the immediate remittance to Pag-IBIG for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees, as warranted by the result of inquiry and reconciliation with HDMF.

We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to  (a) continue to comply with the deduction of Philhealth contributions of its employees and (b) promptly remit all deductions made to Philhealth  pursuant to Section 18(b) of RA No. 7875 and Its Implementing Rules and Regulations, respectively.

We recommended that management continue to withhold taxes and check that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and immediately to BIR within the prescribed period as required by Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 23, 2007.

We recommended that the Cashier / disbursing officer adopt the control procedure of perforating or stamping “PAID”  on all disbursement vouchers and supporting documents immediately after payment to prevent the reuse of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to strengthen internal control over collections by:

a.  strictly adhering to the regulations requiring the deposit of collections daily and intact; and

b.         posting daily entries in the cashbook the collections and deposits pursuant to Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS and ensuring that the balance thereof corresponds to the actual cash in vault by fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 particularly on the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:

a. exerts efforts to provide a central storage room for its supply requirements for stocking purposes; 

b. strictly observes the prescribed accounting guidelines and control procedures on the receipt, issuance, and storage/custodianship of supplies and materials; and 

c) pending the availability of a central storage area for inventories, install adequate controls by maintaining a record of receipt and issuances to the pave way for establishing the cost of ending inventory at the end of the accounting period.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

prioritize the hiring/appointment of a full-time MDRRM Officer;

instruct the Accountant to continue to prepare a certified monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRMF and submit the same to the COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and 

instruct the MDRRM officer to update the LDRRMP from time to time to continuously address current and future disaster risks/hazards.

We recommended that the:

a.  Members of the Municipal Local School Board (i) allocate the SEF budget only for eligible and priority program, projects and activities allowed by the law and by the latest 2017 SEF guidelines under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2017-01; and (ii) encourage schools to make use of the DepEd's funds for regular MOOE in their operations to emphasize that the SEF serves as supplementary budget only or may be availed of only net of the DepEd's allocation;

b.         Members of the Municipal Local School Board and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that all charges to the SEF are within the approved budget and in adherence to the provisions of R.A. 5447, Section 100 and 272, RA 7160 and in conformity with the latest SEF guidelines in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2017-01.

c. Supply Officer of the municipality prepare Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) for the issued equipment and semi-expendable items to the concerned principals and teachers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Development Council:

a. continue to invest in high-impact development projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund so that the best possible desired level of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes may be achieved/attained; and

b. ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close supervision and monitoring to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all respective project implementers and coordinators on the projects funded from fund transfers to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the on-going projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries of the projects.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant with the deduction of GSIS premiums and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant in the withholding of taxes from compensation and payments to suppliers and the remittance thereof within the prescribed time.

require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain Property Records  starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim for a yearly increase of coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances
direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue with the preparation of Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) for PPE items, until all items are covered and thereafter, renew the AREs at least every three years or whenever there is change of custodianship; and

No. 9003;   (b) comply with the submission of the additional requirements specified in the NSWC evaluation checklist for the approval of its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan; (c) instruct the Acting Solid Waste Management Officer to closely supervise and monitor the  operations of the MRFs to sustain the Municipality’s compliant rating from the ECA;  (d) strictly enforce the Sangguniang Bayan ordinance on the “no segregation, no collection” policy.; and (e) strengthen information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangay officials in order to sustain and improve further its compliance rating.

observations.managementAction: Deposit of collections by the Treasurer’s Office was done on a regular basis although there were isolated cases of collection not being deposited intact.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers based on the Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor’s Office starting with active taxpayers and expanding the roll by aiming to increase it annually until all taxpayers are covered; and

Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT/SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and to provide and maintain subsidiary ledgers of individual taxpayer, starting with those active taxpayers, to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant set up at the beginning of the year, the RPT/SET Receivable and the Deferred RPT/SET based on the Certified List of Tax Payers and taxes Due submitted by the Municipal Treasurer. The said list was based on the Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor’s Office. For monitoring purposes, Individual Real Property Tax Account Registers (RPTARs) are prepared and maintained by the Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason:
While the I-Tax System was implemented January 1, 2016, the accounting office does not have a linkage to the system. As such, the preparation of an individual subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer would be difficult.
The LGU, being a first class municipality, has 44 barangays and coupled with the volume of tax payers, reconstruction of the individual subsidiary ledger would be highly improbable. For the record, since CY 2002, not a single subsidiary ledger has been maintained by the Accounting Office.

observations.managementAction: As conveyed during last year’s exit conference, the local chief executive created an Inventory and Appraisal Committee for the conduct of physical inventory of property, plant and equipment (PPE) and appraisal of unbooked parcel of land, respectively. There was a continuous provision of subsidiary ledgers for all current procurements of PPE. Moreover, an initial Inventory Report per department was submitted to the General Service Office as a point of reference in the conduct of physical inventory of PPE.
observations.reason: Conduct of physical inventory of PPE proved to be difficult, hence was not completed, due to incomplete records prior to CY 2015, such as ARE, Equipment Ledger Cards, Property Cards, etc. as regards the unbooked parcels of land, as well as others with titling issues the same are still being verified for evaluation and proper disposition.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require

the Municipal Accountant to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in recording all procured goods to its appropriate inventory account;

the designated supply officer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively for items held in stock at the storage room; and

the requisitioning officer and supply officer to prepare RIS upon release of the items and prepare the SSMI and forward the same to the municipal accountant for the preparation of JEV to record the expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Except for some office supplies, all procurements of inventory were supported with the Inspection and Acceptance Report and Requisition and Issue Slips to signify that the said supplies were issued in their entirety to the end-user departments. Moreover, a stockroom for the supply office alone is provided in the plan of the New Municipal Building, construction of which is ongoing.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is precluded from implementing fully the perpetual inventory method in accounting for procured inventories due to lack of an office space to store the said supplies at the GSO. As such, most of the procurements are issued directly to the requisitioning department.

observations.managementAction: As of the date, DVs, ORs, Trial Balances, Bank Reconciliation Statements etc., covering the period January – November 2016 had been submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the members of     the Bids and Awards Committee, including the BAC Secretariat to – 
render an explanation or justification on the resort to shopping instead of thru competitive bidding in the procurement of the subject contracts;

henceforth, adhere to Sections 48.2 and 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the general policy on competitive bidding and prohibition of splitting of contracts; and

resort only to alternative modes of procurement if the conditions are met as provided under Section 48, 52, and 53.9 of the IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Shopping was resorted to as an alternative mode in the procurement of goods and services pursuant to the limitations contained in RA 9184, as amended.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Services Officer to – 
furnish the Office of the Audit Team Leader copies of Purchase Orders and Contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days from the perfection of the contract for evaluation and review; and

provide copies of delivery receipts to the Office of the Audit Team Leader on a timely and regular basis to allow the inspection of delivered items on a random basis.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Purchase Orders and Delivery Receipts and all supporting documents pertinent thereto were not submitted to the Audit Team Leader within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the –
Municipal Treasurer to strictly follow the regulation on payment through check directly to the recipients/beneficiaries;

Municipal Social Welfare Officer to evaluate and screen properly the recipients by giving priority to the less privileged constituents; and

henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments are supported with complete/proper documentations.
observations.managementAction: Payments regarding Financial / Medical / Burial assistance were made directly to the concerned individuals after having undergone thorough screenings by the social workers/social welfare development officer. An accomplished Intake Sheet / Social Case Study Report supported the payments. Moreover, payments on the grant of financial assistance to the barangay / youth organizations were supported with the proposed program/activity and details of the estimated expenses. This serves as guide of management in ascertaining the reasonableness of the amount granted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board – 
adhere to the provisions of R.A. 5447, Section 100 and 272, RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in the utilization of the Local School Board Fund; and 

encourage schools to make use of the DepEd’s funds for regular MOOE in their operations.
observations.managementAction: Some expenditure charged against the Special Education Fund were not still aligned to the provisions of RAs 5447 and 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 and 1-A.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 particularly on the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Though the municipality created GAD Focal Point System, the municipality still failed to conduct GAD mainstreaming based on gender audit and sex-disaggregated database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply strictly with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan to ensure the optimum implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on the utilization of the LDRRMF, covering the period January – November 2016 had been submitted to the Audit Team Leader.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive –
continue to invest in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund  and refrain from proposing non-capital investment projects under the Fund; and 
ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: There was a continuous investment in high-impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund with a total appropriation of P48,839,793.60 and total expenditures/ commitments of P46,974,927.76, registering a utilization rate of 96.18%.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive, thru the Local Solid Management Board, continue to seek ways and means to improve further the quality of its compliance with RA N. 9003 such as (a) providing financial and/or technical assistance, if necessary, to barangay that cannot afford to construct their own MRFs or are encountering location issues in scouting for sites; and (b) widening further thru visible or conspicuous signage, the awareness of the public on the “No Littering” policy.
observations.managementAction: There was continuous compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003 on the formation and implementation of ecological solid waste management plans and programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality continue to being compliant with the deduction of GSIS premiums and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: There was continuous compliance  on the deduction of GSIS premiums and the remittance of the same within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
members of the BAC and Municipal Accountant evaluate properly the documents and ensure completeness before payment to substantiate the regularity of claims; and

BAC Secretariat comply with the prescribed period on posting of the Notice of Award in the PhilGEPS and require the contractor to submit the warranty security one year from project completion up to final acceptance of the project or the defects liability period.
observations.managementAction: All disbursement for the procurement of infrastructure projects through the public bidding were supported with the documentary requirements and were in accordance with documentary requirements and were in accordance with provisions of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer comply with the policies, rules and procedures on the preparation of the ABC of the DPWH DO No. 22, revising DO No. 72.
observations.managementAction: After revisiting DPWH DO No. 22, the Municipal Engineer complied with the policies rules and procedures on the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the:

Contractor to refund the cost deficiency of the project amounting to P14,263.46; and

Municipal Engineer comply with the Minimum Test Requirements Governing Items of Works as required under the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airports, Volume II, series of 1998.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P14,263.46 representing the value of unaccomplished required volume of work items relative to the FMR project at Barangay Concepcion, that was disallowed in the audit was refunded under and was properly recorded and accounted for in the books. On the other hand, guided by the DPWH standards specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airports, Volume II, series of 1998 the Municipal Engineer complied with the Minimum Test Requirements Governing Items of Works.
